rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the flaw at the memcpy in the bad variant (around line 42) using sizeof(*structCharVoid), which copies the size of the whole struct into charFirst (16 bytes), overflowing into the following pointers. They explained the concrete heap buffer overflow and pointer corruption mechanism.
rationale.local_fix: They gave the exact local change: use sizeof(structCharVoid->charFirst) in memcpy to limit the copy to the destination field size. This directly prevents overwriting the adjacent pointers.
rationale.global_fix: Their global advice (use strncpy or use the size of the destination) is generic and still allows passing the wrong size, and they did not specify a bounded field-copy helper or typed wrapper that prevents confusing struct size with field size. No concrete helper/wrapper implementation was provided.
